violations.text: -Raw animal foods such as shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as cooked stew in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. -Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw beef steaks and raw hamburger patties over live lobster and raw fish). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of pink and black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off during time of inspection. Ice machine to be cleaned and sanitized prior to use. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored with dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
FMC expired, must re-instate. Application provided during time of inspection; food safety certificate current. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2025

1. All handwashing sinks in facility are lacking notification signage. State handout provided during time of inspection 2. Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink on the far end of cookline. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Shellfish tags must include the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. Guidance provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw shelled eggs stored above live littleneck). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

Soup and other prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. -Staff removed the open beverage container from the prep area during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) was observed with food residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the slicer during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023

Rice, kale soup, cacoila, and chicken soup were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. -Staff date marked the TCS foods during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2023
